summary: Frontend-only E2E review of the static portfolio SPA at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All 17 listed checks pass: page loads with 0 console errors, sticky header is transparent at top and gets .glass-header class on scroll, all 5 desktop nav links and the 'Let's Talk' CTA smooth-scroll to their sections, both hero CTAs jump to #projects (scrollY ~5255) and #contact (scrollY ~8397), all 8 sections render in order (hero, about, skills, experience, projects, writing, contact, footer), Skills bento shows 6 capability cards, Experience timeline shows 4 role rows (+1 section node), Projects shows 3 cards with images that load (naturalWidth=1024 once in viewport), Blog shows 3 teasers whose images load (naturalWidth up to 4160), Contact shows the large heading, primary-email-cta and 3 contact rows, Footer 'Back to top' returns scrollY to ~2. Mobile (390x844): hamburger toggle opens/closes the mobile sheet correctly. No horizontal overflow on desktop (1920x1080) or mobile (0px on both). Fonts: Cabinet Grotesk on hero heading, Manrope on body, JetBrains Mono on .overline; document.fonts confirms all three families loaded. All interactive elements expose data-testid.
critical_code_review_comments: Projects/Blog images use loading='lazy' (default behaviour), which is correct; nothing to fix. Just noting that automated snapshots before scrolling will see naturalWidth=0 — not a bug.
Header uses scroll listener to toggle .glass-header — verified working. Consider passive:true on the listener for perf (minor).
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static React + Tailwind + framer-motion portfolio, single page, no backend. All sections use semantic data-testid attributes (site-header, hero-section, about-section, skills-section, experience-section, projects-section, writing-section, contact-section, site-footer; nav-{label}, mobile-nav-{label}, mobile-menu-toggle, header-cta, mobile-cta, hero-cta-projects, hero-cta-contact, back-to-top, primary-email-cta, project-{n}, blog-post-{i}). Images are lazy-loaded — scroll to section before asserting naturalWidth. html { scroll-behavior: smooth } drives all in-page anchor navigation.
